questions.q: The characteristic feature of plant cell is the presence of:
The process of budding in plants is an example of:
Which of the following is not a type of plant tissue?
Explain the importance of stomata in the life of plants.
questions.answer: Stomata are small pores found on the surface of leaves and stems of plants. They play a crucial role in the exchange of gases, such as carbon dioxide and oxygen, and water vapour.

questions.q: Distinguish between monocot and dicot roots.
questions.answer: Monocot roots have a single cotyledon and roots are fibrous, e.g., wheat. Dicot roots have two cotyledons and roots are tap roots, e.g., pea.

questions.q: State the function of cuticle in plant.
questions.answer: prevents water loss from the plant

questions.q: What is the difference between gymnosperms and angiosperms?
questions.answer: Gymnosperms produce seeds without flowers, e.g., pine. Angiosperms produce seeds within flowers, e.g., pea.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic feature of monocot stem?
Explain the significance of root hairs in plant.
questions.answer: Root hairs increase the surface area of root for absorption of water and minerals from the soil.

questions.q: Distinguish between exogenous and endogenous growth.
questions.answer: Exogenous growth is growth due to external factors, e.g., increase in length of stem due to auxin. Endogenous growth is growth due to internal factors, e.g., increase in thickness of stem due to cell division.
